name: SCC_TLSHandshakeInd
title: SCC_TLSHandshakeInd
syntax: void SCC_TLSHandshakeInd( long Status, long IsServer)
description: The callback is called when a TLS handshake has been attempted after the SECC Discovery procedure. It is not called, however, if TLS is disabled or TCP was negotiated during SECC Discovery.
To enable TLS, call SCC_SetTLSEnabled(1) in CAPL or set the parameter <UseTLS> to 1 in the XML configuration file.

parameters.name: Status
parameters.description: 1 – TLS handshake has successfully been completed. 2 – TLS handshake has failed. The Write window may contain more details about the cause of the failure. 3 – TLS handshake has most likey failed due to a bad certificate.

parameters.name: IsServer
parameters.description: 0 – Callback for EV. 1 – Callback for EVSE. You may usually ignore this parameter as your CAPL code is linked to the EV or EVSE simulation DLL.

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units
